root_motivation: Close the off-device gap in wintermute so family messages actually reach Joe and the dialog FSM stops timing out into 'I couldn't reach Joe.'
user_persona: jsy running wintermute on a headless Linux box; family members speak requests to the ambient system which must relay them off-device to Joe's phone or email in under a minute.

acceptance_criteria.description: wm-reach --help lists daemon, send, reply, test-transport subcommands.
Email transport with WM_REACH_SENDMAIL fake delivers body.
wm.family.distress delivered before queued normal message.
ntfy/webhook behind Cargo features, excluded from default build.
Self-emitted topic filter prevents daemon re-consuming own acks.
systemd unit ExecStart matches install path (no .cargo/bin drift).
No secret in logs; config read from /etc/wintermute/conf.d/.
cargo test green; cargo clippy clean; release gate receipts produced.

scope: Reply subcommand for v1 inbound stub
Daemon, send, reply, test-transport CLI surface
non_goals: No inbound email polling in v1
No routing logic (that's the dialog FSM)

additional.no_network_in_tests: All tests use fake sendmail scripts or in-process mock transports; no real SMTP or ntfy connections in cargo test
additional.no_secret_in_logs: Transport credentials must never appear in any log line, panic, or Debug output

five_whys_trace.q: Why does wintermute need an off-device transport daemon?
five_whys_trace.a: No daemon today can cross the local/off-device boundary; family messages time out into 'I couldn't reach Joe.'

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the dialog time out without this?
five_whys_trace.a: PRD-wintermute-family-intents enters FamilyPending and waits for wm.family.ack; without this daemon the ack never arrives.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not have the dialog FSM publish to an external API directly?
five_whys_trace.a: The transport choice should be swappable without touching the dialog FSM; isolation to one daemon is the right abstraction.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does distress need a privileged bypass path?
five_whys_trace.a: wm.family.distress is published on its own topic so a delivery daemon can treat it as bypass-batching, highest-priority.
